draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners want real-time location feedback that feels alive and responsive, not static map markers
facade.axisTargeted: MOTION AND BEHAVIOR — animated feedback vs static positioning
facade.label: GPS dot pulses
facade.acceptImplies: User values continuous visual feedback and kinetic reassurance during runs; map interface should use breathing/pulsing animations for current position
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers minimal, settled interface; static map markers are sufficient; animated elements feel distracting or wasteful during physical activity

evidence.hypothesis: Runners want real-time location feedback that feels alive and responsive, not static map markers
evidence.implication: User values continuous visual feedback and kinetic reassurance during runs; map interface should use breathing/pulsing animations for current position

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer low-distraction interfaces with high contrast for visibility in bright sunlight
facade.label: Dark and minimal
facade.acceptImplies: Build with dark mode as primary, sparse layouts, high-contrast text for outdoor readability
facade.rejectImplies: Explore lighter palettes or more information density per screen

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar showing live pace, distance, and time—always visible while scanning weather—reduces cognitive load vs. tabbed switching
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation & information hierarchy: persistent sidebar vs. tab-based layout
facade.label: Sidebar with running stats
facade.acceptImplies: User values at-a-glance run metrics without mode-switching; app layout should prioritize continuous data visibility over screen real estate
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers compact, focused views; weather is primary; run stats should be secondary/modal/collapsed; tabs or drawer may be preferred to a static sidebar

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners benefits from an upbeat, personality-driven tone—like a supportive AI coach—rather than a neutral, clinical weather service.
facade.label: Cheerful robot voice
facade.acceptImplies: Users want personality and motivation in their running companion. Expect embracing of character, humor, and emotional support in copy and tone.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a clean, trustworthy, no-nonsense voice. They want facts fast without personality overhead. Design toward clarity and credibility over charm.

facade.hypothesis: Runners prioritize live metrics (pace, distance, time) overlaid directly on the map view rather than hidden in separate panels or post-run summaries.
facade.axisTargeted: Information density vs. cognitive load — should key data be always-visible and integrated, or reserved and compartmentalized?
facade.label: Run stats inline
facade.acceptImplies: Embed live numbers into the running interface itself. Stack metrics as a persistent semi-transparent bar or floating card. Accept that visual clutter during the run is worth immediate feedback.
facade.rejectImplies: Defer metrics to post-run screens, summaries, or swipeable panels. Keep the map clean and primary. Data as reward, not companion.

facade.hypothesis: A running weather app might balance digital precision (metrics, temperature, wind speed) with warm, encouraging visual treatment—using luminous color and rounded typography to make cold data feel approachable and motivating rather than clinical.
facade.label: Glowing data numbers
facade.acceptImplies: User wants the app to feel human-centered and emotionally engaging, not sterile. Preference for warmth bleeding into function. Likely accepts ambient/glowing UI, softer iconography, conversational tone.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, minimal data presentation—crisp numbers, high contrast, no atmospheric effects. Likely leans toward stark vs. cozy, wants information density and clarity over aesthetic warmth.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners need animated feedback that mirrors their cadence and effort level, turning static data into kinetic feedback that feels embodied
facade.axisTargeted: Motion intensity: does the app match user energy or stay neutral?
facade.label: Pace indicator throbs
facade.acceptImplies: Design should sync micro-animations (progress bars, stats updates, notifications) to running rhythm; motion should feel biological, not mechanical
facade.rejectImplies: Motion should be decoupled from user state; animations are decorative or constant-tempo, not responsive to effort or speed

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:
